sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: COVID-19 rapid guideline: managing the long-term effects of COVID-19
sources.version: NICE guideline NG188; last updated 25 January 2024
sources.jurisdiction: United Kingdom; international evidence requiring UAE pathway adaptation
sources.population: Adults, children and young people with new or ongoing symptoms four weeks or more after suspected or confirmed acute COVID-19.
sources.clinical_setting: Any healthcare setting, including primary and community care, with recommendation-specific remote, in-person and post-discharge qualifiers.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for long-COVID identification and holistic symptom review documentation; diagnosis, investigations, referral, management and UAE service pathways remain clinician-led and locally governed.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1 and 1.5–1.10 — case definition, initial consultation, assessment need, and follow-up context
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician documentation of suspected or confirmed acute-COVID context, symptom duration from four weeks, fluctuating symptoms, initial consultation, functional change, further-assessment need and selected follow-up context without establishing diagnosis automatically.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 2.1–2.6 — holistic person-centred assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording acute-COVID history, current and previous symptom nature, severity, timing, duration, comorbidity, physical, cognitive, psychological, psychiatric and functional impact, and patient concerns without predicting course or generating management.
